canonical_question: What authentication methods does SAP S/4HANA support (OAuth, X.509, SAML) by deployment model?
aliases: SAP S/4HANA authentication options by deployment model
How to authenticate API calls to SAP S/4HANA Cloud and on-premise
SAP OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer vs Client Credentials for S/4HANA

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2025-Q3 — Basic Auth deprecated for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition inbound APIs

constraints: Basic Authentication is deprecated for inbound API access on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
OAuth 2.0 on S/4HANA Cloud only supports Client Credentials and SAML Bearer Assertion grant types
X.509 client certificates for S/4HANA Cloud must be CA-signed — self-signed certificates are rejected
Kerberos/SPNEGO not available on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
SAP is migrating from XSUAA to IAS — all new BTP apps should use IAS
Principal propagation requires pre-established trust between sender and receiver systems

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs Salesforce or Oracle ERP authentication methods
User needs SAP BTP application security (not S/4HANA backend)
User needs SAP authorization/role model (not authentication)

related_to.label: SAP S/4HANA OData API capabilities and rate limits

often_confused_with.label: SAP BTP application security (XSUAA/IAS) — platform-level, not backend

inputs_needed.question: Which SAP S/4HANA deployment model are you using?
What type of integration authentication do you need?
What is your security requirement level?
